summary: Fire‑filled set with standout Candyman, Estimated>Eyes and Bertha
blurb: The Boston Music Hall show bursts with high‑octane energy – Jerry’s solos on Candyman and Sugaree blaze, while the second set rolls through a scorching Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World stretch. Keith’s aggressive keyboards and tight drum work keep the momentum nonstop.
review: On November 14, 1978 at Boston Music Hall the Dead were absolutely on fire. Jerry Garcia drove the band with incendiary leads on Candyman, Sugaree and Stagger Lee, delivering what reviewers called “over‑drive” and “elite” playing. Keith Godchaux stepped out front, his aggressive, front‑and‑center keys lifting every jam, especially through the soaring Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World sequence that kept the second set on a relentless roll. Donna Jean’s vocals added extra sparkle on From The Heart Of Me while Bob Weir tightened the groove after an unusually fast Sugaree, and the twin drummers provided a crisp, tight “short but sweet” drum‑and‑space segment that propelled the band into a raucous NFA → Good Lovin' → Johnny B. Goode finale. The audience tapes capture crystal‑clear ambience and full‑band mix, making this one of the most vivid live documents of the 1978 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are uniformly positive, praising Jerry’s incendiary solos, Keith’s aggressive keyboards, and the overall high‑energy flow. Multiple reviewers highlight the same standout songs (Candyman, Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World, Bertha) and describe the performance as "smoking," "elite," and "on fire," indicating strong consensus of exceptional playing.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear audience tape on a Nakamichi 300 that captures the full band mix, vivid crowd ambience and all of the show’s fire‑filled highlights.

key_highlights: Jerry’s over‑driven solos on Candyman and Sugaree drive the set.
Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World sequence is a soaring second‑set centerpiece.
Keith’s aggressive, front‑and‑center keyboard work lifts the jams.
Tight drum and space segment provides perfect flow into the raucous finale.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is in overdrive, delivering standout solos on Candyman, Sugaree and Stagger Lee that reviewers call "smoking" and "elite."
band_performance.Bob: Weir tightens the rhythm after a fast Sugaree, helping the band regain its groove.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboard work is aggressive and front‑and‑center, with tasteful liftoffs that elevate the entire set.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums (alongside Billy) provide a short but sweet segment praised for tightness and flow.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey the drum duo delivers crisp, driving rhythms that keep the momentum high.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
